The date this volunteer participated in the assessment fieldwork. May differ from assessment_date if the assessment spanned multiple days and this volunteer only participated on specific days. Supports attendance tracking and per-diem calculation.

Current status of this volunteer's participation in this assessment. Tracks lifecycle from recruitment through completion. Values: confirmed (volunteer committed), completed (fieldwork done), cancelled (volunteer withdrew), no_show (volunteer did not appear). Supports operational coordination and volunteer reliability tracking.

Number of individual survey submissions, interviews, or FGD sessions this volunteer completed during this assessment. Used for productivity tracking, workload balancing, and incentive calculation. This is assessment-specific—cannot be stored on volunteer (varies by assessment) or assessment (varies by volunteer).

Status indicating whether this volunteer completed the methodology-specific training required for this assessment (e.g., MUAC screening training, PDM interview protocol, KoboToolbox form training). Values: completed (training done), not_required (volunteer already certified), pending (scheduled), incomplete (did not complete). This is assessment-specific because different assessment types require different training.

description: Unique identifier for the beneficiary exit record. Primary key for the exit_record product.

Classification determining how beneficiary data should be handled post-exit per data minimization and privacy requirements. Retain full means keep all data for ongoing monitoring; retain anonymized means remove PII but keep aggregated data; archive means move to long-term storage; delete means purge per right to be forgotten.
reference: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Article 5, Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS)

description: Overall outcome classification from the exit assessment. Positive indicates beneficiary achieved program objectives; neutral indicates partial achievement; negative indicates unmet objectives; not applicable for exits without assessment (death, loss of contact).

Method by which exit consent was obtained from the beneficiary. Verbal for in-person oral consent; written for signed paper forms; digital for electronic signature; proxy for consent obtained through authorized representative.

Boolean flag indicating whether explicit consent was obtained from the beneficiary for the exit and associated data handling. Required for voluntary withdrawals and data deletion per GDPR and CHS standards.

Human-readable business identifier for the exit record, formatted as EXT- followed by 8 digits. Used for tracking and reference in case management systems.

Primary category describing why the beneficiary is exiting the registry. Program graduation indicates successful completion of assistance; voluntary withdrawal is beneficiary-initiated; relocation means moved out of service area; death is confirmed mortality; loss of contact means unable to reach beneficiary; loss of eligibility means no longer meets program criteria.

Detailed narrative explanation of the specific circumstances surrounding the beneficiary exit. Provides context beyond the category for case management and Monitoring Evaluation and Learning (MEL) purposes.

Current workflow status of the exit record. Pending indicates awaiting supervisor approval; approved means exit authorized but not yet finalized; completed means exit fully processed and beneficiary deactivated; reversed means exit was cancelled and beneficiary reactivated.
Precise timestamp when the exit record was created in the system, capturing the exact moment of registry deactivation.

Boolean flag indicating whether this exit was triggered by a duplicate record merge operation. True means this record was identified as a duplicate and merged into another master record; false means exit was for other reasons.
Timestamp when this exit record was last modified in the data lakehouse. Updated whenever any attribute value changes. Part of standard audit trail.
Scheduled date for the next post-exit follow-up contact or assessment. Used to track ongoing monitoring obligations for graduated beneficiaries.

Boolean flag indicating whether post-exit follow-up monitoring is required for this beneficiary. Typically true for program graduation cases to assess sustainability of outcomes per Results-Based Management (RBM) frameworks.

Boolean flag indicating whether the beneficiary was provided with referrals to other services or organizations at exit. Part of responsible exit protocol per Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) commitment to do no harm.

description: Classifies the registrant as an individual, household head, household member, or community-level registration. Determines the unit of analysis for program targeting, NFI distribution, and beneficiary counting methodologies.
reference: UNHCR proGres v4 Registration Type; WFP SCOPE Beneficiary Classification; Sphere Association Standards

description: Biological sex of the registrant as recorded at registration. Used for DAC-compliant gender disaggregation in MEL reporting, GBV protection screening, and SPHERE-aligned needs assessments.
reference: OECD DAC Gender Equality Marker; UNHCR Age and Gender Disaggregation Policy; IASC Gender Handbook

description: The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., CommCare case_id, KoboToolbox submission UUID, proGres v4 individual_id). Enables bidirectional traceability and re-synchronization with operational systems.
reference: CommCare Case Management API; KoboToolbox Submission API; UNHCR proGres v4 Data Dictionary

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this registrant record in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The document number from the identity verification document presented at registration (e.g., national ID number, passport number, UNHCR card number). Stored encrypted; used for deduplication and re-registration identity matching.
reference: UNHCR Data Protection Guidelines; GDPR Article 9; USAID BHA Beneficiary Verification Requirements

description: Type of identity verification document presented at registration. Used for identity assurance scoring, fraud prevention, and compliance with donor KYB (Know Your Beneficiary) requirements. 'Community attestation' applies where formal documents are unavailable.
reference: UNHCR Registration Guidelines; USAID BHA Beneficiary Verification Requirements; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Categorical classification of the registrant's vulnerability level derived from the vulnerability_score. Drives program targeting decisions, priority service access, and donor reporting on most-vulnerable population coverage.
reference: UNHCR Vulnerability Assessment Framework; OCHA Humanitarian Needs Overview; DAC Evaluation Criteria

description: Composite vulnerability index score calculated from the needs assessment, capturing food insecurity, protection risks, health status, and displacement severity. Used for program prioritization, targeting, and MEL outcome tracking. Score range and methodology defined per program LogFrame.
reference: UNHCR Vulnerability Assessment Framework; WFP Vulnerability Analysis and Mapping (VAM); IASC Needs Assessment Standards

description: Unique identifier for the beneficiary registration event. Primary key for the registration event record.
Reference to the humanitarian or development program under which this registration was conducted.

Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Registration events are held at specific field sites. Direct business relationship for event management, site capacity planning, and registration campaign tracking. Replaces denormalized location name
Reference to the beneficiary who was registered or re-registered in this event.
Reference to the staff member or field worker who conducted the registration session.
Indicates whether biometric data (fingerprint, iris scan, facial recognition) was captured during this registration event.
reference: UNHCR Biometric Identity Management System (BIMS) Standards

description: Type of biometric data captured during registration: fingerprint, iris scan, facial recognition, or none.
reference: UNHCR Biometric Identity Management System (BIMS) Standards

description: Date on which informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary.
Indicates whether informed consent for data collection and processing was obtained from the beneficiary during registration.
Type of consent obtained: verbal, written, digital signature, or guardian consent (for minors or vulnerable individuals).
reference: GDPR Article 7, UNHCR Data Protection Policy

description: Timestamp when this registration event record was first created in the data system.
Assessment of the overall data quality for this registration event: high, medium, low, or needs review.
reference: Monitoring Evaluation Accountability and Learning (MEAL) Framework

description: Name of the source system or application from which this registration event data was extracted (e.g., CommCare, KoboToolbox, UNHCR PRIMES).
Indicates whether a deduplication check was performed during registration to prevent duplicate beneficiary records.
Method used for deduplication check: biometric matching, demographic matching, document number verification, manual review, or none.
Indicates whether a potential duplicate beneficiary record was identified during the deduplication check.
Status of duplicate resolution: no duplicate found, duplicate confirmed and merged, duplicate resolved, or pending manual review.
Indicates whether the registered beneficiary is designated as the head of household for program purposes.
Indicates whether this registration event was part of a household-level registration (true) or individual-only registration (false).
Indicates whether an interpreter was used during the registration session due to language barriers.
Timestamp when this registration event record was last updated or modified in the data system.
Indicates whether the beneficiary requires referral to specialized services (protection, health, psychosocial support) based on registration screening.

Type of referral service required: protection, health, psychosocial support (PSS), legal aid, livelihood, education, or other specialized services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protection|health|pss|legal_aid|livelihood|education|nutrition|wash|shelter|none — promote to reference product]
Name of the organization (INGO, CBO, CSO) that conducted the registration, relevant for consortium or partnership programs.
Full name of the staff member or field worker who conducted the registration for audit and accountability purposes.
Percentage score (0-100) indicating the completeness of data captured during registration based on required and optional fields.
The date on which the beneficiary registration or re-registration event occurred.
ISO 639-3 three-letter language code indicating the language used during the registration interview or data collection.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the registration location in decimal degrees format.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the registration location in decimal degrees format.
The modality or method through which the registration was conducted: in-person at center, mobile outreach, remote digital, phone interview, or community-based registration.
Free-text field for registering staff to capture additional observations, special circumstances, or contextual information about the registration event.
Externally visible unique registration number assigned to the beneficiary during this event. Format: Country-Year-Sequence (e.g., SYR-2024-000123).
Current status of the registration event in the workflow: draft, pending verification, verified, approved, rejected, or incomplete.
Precise timestamp when the registration session was initiated, including time zone information.
The data collection tool or system used to capture registration information: KoboToolbox, CommCare, paper form, UNHCR PRIMES, custom application, or other.
reference: ICT4D (Information and Communication Technology for Development) Standards

description: Type of registration event: initial registration, re-registration, profile update, verification visit, or biometric enrollment.
The document number or identifier from the verification document presented during registration.
reference: GDPR Article 4, UNHCR Data Protection Policy

description: Type of identity or verification document presented during registration (e.g., national ID, passport, birth certificate, ration card, IDP card). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: national_id|passport|birth_certificate|ration_card|idp_card|refugee_certificate|voter_id|driver_license|none — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether a vulnerability or needs assessment was conducted as part of this registration event.
Unique system-generated identifier for the vulnerability profile record. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance — Beneficiary Data Management

description: Reference to the household unit associated with this vulnerability profile. Enables household-level aggregation and targeting for program interventions.
reference: UNHCR Household Registration Guidelines; KoboToolbox Household Survey Module

description: Reference to the primary humanitarian or development program under which this vulnerability profile was generated or is being used for targeting. Links the profile to program-specific eligibility criteria and Theory of Change (ToC) outcomes.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module; IATI Activity Standard

description: Reference to the immediately preceding vulnerability profile record for this registrant, enabling longitudinal tracking of vulnerability changes over time. Populated when profile_status is 'superseded' or when a new profile replaces an existing one.
reference: IATI Data Standard — Activity History; CommCare Case History

description: Reference to the individual beneficiary or household head registered in the beneficiary registry. Links this vulnerability profile to the specific Person of Concern (PoC) or Internally Displaced Person (IDP) record.
reference: UNHCR Beneficiary Registration Standards; CommCare Case Management

description: Reference to the staff member or field worker who conducted the vulnerability assessment. Used for quality assurance, accountability, and inter-rater reliability analysis in MEL processes.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance — Accountability to Affected Populations; HAP Standards

description: Date on which the most recent vulnerability assessment was conducted that materially changed or established this profile. Used for timeliness tracking and MEL reporting cycles.
reference: SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; OCHA Needs Assessment Guidelines

description: Data collection tool or platform used to conduct the vulnerability assessment that generated this profile. Supports data quality tracking and source system lineage. Aligned with operational systems including KoboToolbox and CommCare.
reference: KoboToolbox Field Data Collection; CommCare Mobile Case Management; ICT4D Standards

description: Number of children under 5 years of age in the household. Children under 5 are the primary target group for acute malnutrition screening (MUAC), vaccination campaigns, and early childhood development programs. Used for nutrition and child protection program sizing.
reference: UNICEF Child Nutrition Standards; SPHERE Nutrition Standards; SMART Methodology

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has one or more documented chronic illnesses (e.g., HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, diabetes, hypertension) that increase vulnerability and require sustained health service access. Drives health program targeting and DHIS2 health indicator reporting.
reference: WHO International Classification of Diseases (ICD-11); DHIS2 Health Program Monitoring

description: Coded description of the primary chronic illness condition affecting the beneficiary (e.g., HIV_AIDS, tuberculosis, diabetes, hypertension, malnutrition_chronic). Populated only when chronic_illness_flag is True. Aligned with ICD-11 coding where applicable.
reference: WHO ICD-11; DHIS2 Health Program Monitoring; PEPFAR Reporting Standards

description: Aggregated multi-dimensional vulnerability score derived from weighted combination of food insecurity (IPC phase), protection risk, disability, nutritional status, displacement category, and other assessed indicators. Used for program targeting, beneficiary prioritization, and Results-Based Management (RBM) reporting. Score range typically 0.00–100.00.
reference: OCHA Humanitarian Needs Overview (HNO) Methodology; IASC Needs Assessment Framework; RBM Standards

description: Date on which informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or their legal guardian. Required for data protection compliance and audit trail purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ICRC Data Protection in Humanitarian Action; CHS Alliance

description: Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or their legal guardian prior to data collection and vulnerability profiling. Mandatory for compliance with data protection regulations and CHS accountability standards.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; GDPR Article 6; ICRC Data Protection in Humanitarian Action

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the beneficiary is located. Used for multi-country program management, IATI reporting, and donor country-level disaggregation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IATI Data Standard; OCHA ReliefWeb

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has consented to their vulnerability data being shared with partner organizations and inter-agency coordination bodies (e.g., UNHCR, OCHA clusters). Governs data sharing under inter-agency data sharing agreements.
reference: ICRC Data Protection in Humanitarian Action; OCHA Data Responsibility Guidelines; GDPR Article 6

description: Functional difficulty domain identified using the Washington Group Short Set on Functioning (WG-SS) questions. Captures the primary domain of disability: seeing, hearing, mobility, cognition, self-care, or communication. Used for inclusive programming and disability-disaggregated reporting per CRPD. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|seeing|hearing|mobility|cognition|self_care|communication|multiple — promote to reference product]
reference: Washington Group on Disability Statistics (WG-SS); UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (CRPD); IASC Disability Inclusion Guidelines

description: Severity of functional difficulty as measured by the Washington Group Short Set (WG-SS) response scale: no difficulty, some difficulty, a lot of difficulty, or cannot do at all. Used alongside disability_classification for disaggregated disability reporting.
reference: Washington Group on Disability Statistics (WG-SS); UN CRPD

description: Classification of the beneficiary's displacement status per UNHCR definitions. Internally Displaced Person (IDP) refers to those displaced within their country; refugee refers to those who have crossed international borders; returnee refers to those who have returned to their place of origin. Drives program eligibility and UNHCR/OCHA reporting.
reference: UNHCR Refugee Status Determination Guidelines; OCHA IDP Tracking Matrix (DTM)

description: Indicates whether the household includes one or more elderly members (typically aged 60+). Elderly individuals are a recognized vulnerable group requiring targeted assistance per SPHERE and IASC age and disability inclusion guidelines.
reference: SPHERE Standards; IASC Age and Disability Inclusion Guidelines; HelpAge International Standards

description: Indicates whether the household is headed by a woman. Female-headed households are recognized as a priority vulnerability category in humanitarian programming due to heightened protection risks and reduced access to resources. Used for gender-disaggregated reporting.
reference: IASC Gender Handbook; USAID Gender Equality Policy; SDG 5 (Gender Equality)

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has been identified as a survivor or at-risk individual for Gender-Based Violence (GBV). Triggers referral to GBV case management services and specialized support programs. Handled with strict confidentiality per GBV information management protocols.
reference: IASC GBV Guidelines; UNFPA GBV Information Management System (GBVIMS); CHS Alliance

description: Standardized code identifying the geographic area (admin level 2 or 3) where the beneficiary is located at the time of assessment. Used for geographic targeting, GIS mapping, and OCHA cluster reporting. Aligned with OCHA Common Operational Datasets (COD) administrative boundary codes.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets (COD); GADM Administrative Boundaries; Humanitarian OpenStreetMap

business_glossary_term: Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) Phase
description: Food insecurity severity classification using the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC) scale: 1=Minimal, 2=Stressed, 3=Crisis, 4=Emergency, 5=Famine/Catastrophe. Used for food assistance targeting and donor reporting to BHA/USAID.
reference: IPC Global Partners — IPC Technical Manual v3.1; FEWS NET

description: Assessment of the beneficiary household's livelihood and income-generating capacity. 'none' indicates complete loss of livelihood; 'disrupted' indicates significant reduction; 'partial' indicates some income sources remain; 'stable' indicates adequate livelihood. Used for cash and voucher assistance (CVA) targeting.
reference: SEEP Network Livelihoods Assessment Framework; USAID BHA Livelihoods Programming

description: Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) measurement in millimetres, used as the primary anthropometric indicator for acute malnutrition screening in children under 5 and pregnant/lactating women. Thresholds: <115mm = SAM, 115–124mm = MAM, ≥125mm = normal. Collected via KoboToolbox or CommCare field surveys.
reference: WHO MUAC Measurement Protocol; SPHERE Nutrition Standards; SMART Methodology

description: Scheduled date for the next vulnerability reassessment to ensure the profile reflects the beneficiary's current vulnerability state. Drives field operations scheduling and MEL monitoring cycles. Typically set based on program-specific reassessment frequency protocols.
reference: SPHERE Standards; IASC Needs Assessment Framework; MEAL Framework

description: Free-text field for field officers to record contextual observations, qualitative vulnerability factors, or case-specific notes that are not captured by structured indicators. Handled as confidential due to potential sensitivity of content.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; CommCare Case Notes

description: Nutritional status classification based on anthropometric screening. Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) indicates MUAC < 115mm or WHZ < -3; Moderate Acute Malnutrition (MAM) indicates MUAC 115–124mm or WHZ -2 to -3; normal indicates adequate nutritional status. Used for therapeutic feeding program targeting and Global Acute Malnutrition (GAM) rate reporting.
reference: WHO/UNICEF/WFP/UNHCR Joint Statement on SAM; SPHERE Nutrition Standards; SMART Methodology

description: Indicates whether the household includes a pregnant or lactating woman (PLW). PLWs are a priority group for nutrition programming (supplementary feeding), antenatal care, and maternal health services. Used for CMAM and nutrition program targeting.
reference: WHO Nutrition Guidelines for PLW; SPHERE Nutrition Standards; UNICEF CMAM Protocol

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this vulnerability profile record, used in field operations, donor reporting, and inter-agency data sharing (e.g., VP-SOM-000123456).
reference: IATI Data Standard; OCHA Common Operational Datasets

description: Timestamp when this vulnerability profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: IATI Data Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Indicates the operational process or event that triggered the creation or update of this vulnerability profile. 'post_distribution_monitoring' refers to Post-Distribution Monitoring (PDM) assessments; 'emergency_screening' refers to rapid assessments during acute crises. Used for MEL data quality analysis.
reference: OCHA Needs Assessment Guidelines; IASC Operational Guidance on PDM; MEAL Framework

description: Current lifecycle state of the vulnerability profile record. 'active' indicates the current consolidated vulnerability state; 'superseded' indicates a newer profile has replaced this one; 'pending_review' indicates awaiting validation by a protection or MEL officer.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance; CommCare Case Status Lifecycle

description: Timestamp when this vulnerability profile was last modified, reflecting the most recent update to any vulnerability indicator. Triggers downstream re-targeting and prioritization workflows.
reference: IATI Data Standard; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance

description: Assessed level of protection risk for the individual or household, encompassing threats of violence, exploitation, abuse, and discrimination. Classified by a trained protection officer. Drives case prioritization and referral pathways per UNHCR Protection Framework.
reference: UNHCR Protection Framework; IASC Protection Cluster Guidelines; CHS Alliance

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has been assessed as requiring Psychosocial Support (PSS) services, including mental health support, trauma counselling, or community-based psychosocial activities. Triggers referral to PSS programming.
reference: IASC Guidelines on Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS); WHO mhGAP

description: Assessment of the beneficiary's shelter situation against SPHERE minimum standards. 'none' indicates no shelter; 'inadequate' indicates shelter below minimum standards; 'transitional' indicates temporary/emergency shelter; 'adequate' indicates shelter meeting minimum standards. Drives NFI and shelter program targeting.
reference: SPHERE Shelter and Settlement Standards; UNHCR Shelter Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary is an unaccompanied or separated child (UASC) — a child under 18 separated from both parents and other relatives and not being cared for by a responsible adult. Triggers child protection case management and family tracing and reunification (FTR) services.
reference: UNHCR/UNICEF Guidelines on Unaccompanied and Separated Children; CRC Article 20; IASC Child Protection

description: Categorical tier derived from the composite vulnerability score, used for operational targeting and prioritization decisions. 'critical' indicates immediate life-saving intervention required; 'high' indicates priority program enrollment; 'medium' and 'low' indicate standard program eligibility. Distinct from composite_vulnerability_score which is the numeric value.
reference: OCHA HNO Severity Classification; IASC Needs Assessment Framework

business_glossary_term: Water Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) Access Flag
description: Indicates whether the beneficiary household has adequate access to safe water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) facilities meeting SPHERE minimum standards. False indicates a WASH vulnerability requiring intervention. Used for WASH sector targeting and OCHA cluster reporting.
reference: SPHERE WASH Standards; UNICEF/WHO Joint Monitoring Programme (JMP); OCHA WASH Cluster

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each needs assessment record. Primary key for the needs_assessment data product within the beneficiary domain.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Alliance — Data Management Principles

description: Foreign key linking to supply.commodity. Business justification: Needs assessments identify specific commodity requirements (NFI kits, food baskets, WASH items, shelter materials) for beneficiaries. Essential for humanitarian needs-based procurement planning and Sp

Foreign key linking to communication.community_engagement_event. Business justification: Needs assessments are frequently conducted during community engagement events (focus group discussions, community consultations, participatory assessments). Links assessment data to the event context,
Reference to the community or settlement unit that is the subject of this assessment when assessment_level is 'community'. Nullable when assessment_level is 'individual' or 'household'.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets — Settlement/Community Registry

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action_plan. Business justification: When internal reviews or audits identify needs assessment quality issues (sampling bias, incomplete data, targeting errors), corrective action plans document remediation measures. Essential for qualit

The unique identifier of the data collection instrument used to conduct this assessment. For KoboToolbox, this is the form UID (e.g., 'aXmK3p9QrT'). For CommCare, this is the module ID. Enables traceability back to the specific questionnaire version and supports data quality audits.
reference: KoboToolbox Form Management — Form UID; CommCare Application Builder — Module ID

description: Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Volunteers conduct needs assessments in many NGO operations (household surveys, vulnerability assessments, rapid assessments). Standard practice for community-based data collection. Required for track
Reference to the household unit that is the subject of this assessment when assessment_level is 'household'. Nullable when assessment_level is 'individual' or 'community'.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Household-Level Assessment Standards; KoboToolbox Household Survey Module

description: Reference to the humanitarian or development program under which this needs assessment was commissioned. Links the assessment to the program's Theory of Change (ToC) and LogFrame targeting criteria.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Program Management Module; IATI Standard v2.03

description: Foreign key linking to partnership.partner_org. Business justification: Partners conduct needs assessments in their operational areas under consortium or sub-award arrangements. Tracking the assessing partner is required for data quality oversight, capacity assessment val
Reference to the staff member or volunteer who conducted the field assessment. Used for quality assurance, inter-rater reliability checks, and enumerator performance monitoring.
reference: CHS Alliance — Accountability to Affected Populations; CommCare Field Worker Module

description: Reference to the individual beneficiary (IDP, PoC, GBV survivor, or other target population member) who is the subject of this assessment when assessment_level is 'individual'. Nullable when assessment_level is 'household' or 'community'.
reference: CommCare Case Management — Beneficiary Case ID; UNHCR PoC Registration Standards

description: Name of the first-level administrative division (e.g., province, state, region) where the assessment was conducted. Sourced from OCHA Common Operational Datasets (CODs) administrative boundary reference. Used for geographic aggregation in SitReps and donor reports.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets (CODs) — Administrative Boundaries; IATI Country/Region Codes

description: Name of the second-level administrative division (e.g., district, county, department) where the assessment was conducted. Sourced from OCHA CODs. Enables sub-national geographic disaggregation for targeting and cluster coordination reporting.
reference: OCHA Common Operational Datasets (CODs) — Administrative Boundaries

description: The calendar date on which the needs assessment was physically conducted in the field. This is the principal real-world event date, distinct from the record submission or validation timestamps. Used for temporal targeting analysis and periodic reassessment scheduling.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Needs Assessment Timing; OCHA Assessment Registry Standards

description: The unit of analysis at which this needs assessment was conducted. 'individual' targets a single beneficiary (e.g., GBV survivor, SAM case); 'household' targets a family unit; 'community' targets a settlement or geographic cluster. Determines which of beneficiary_id, household_id, or community_id is populated.
reference: SPHERE Handbook Chapter 1 — Needs Assessment Methodology; IASC Needs Assessment Task Force Guidelines

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this needs assessment, used in field reports, SitReps, and donor communications. Format: NA-{COUNTRY}-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Sourced from KoboToolbox or CommCare submission ID.
reference: IATI Standard v2.03 — Activity Identifier; KoboToolbox Form Submission Reference

description: Current workflow lifecycle state of the needs assessment record. 'draft' indicates field data entry in progress; 'submitted' indicates enumerator submission pending review; 'validated' indicates supervisor-approved and usable for targeting; 'rejected' indicates data quality failure requiring re-assessment.
reference: CHS Alliance — Data Quality Standards; KoboToolbox Submission Workflow

description: Version number or deployment date of the assessment tool/form used. Critical for longitudinal comparability — when a KoboToolbox form or CommCare module is updated, findings from different versions may not be directly comparable without version-aware analysis.
reference: KoboToolbox Form Versioning; IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Comparability Standards

description: Classification of the assessment's purpose and methodology. 'initial_registration' is conducted at first beneficiary contact; 'periodic_reassessment' is a scheduled follow-up to update vulnerability status; 'post_crisis_rapid' is an emergency rapid assessment following a shock event; 'sector_specific_deep' is an in-depth assessment for a single sector (e.g., nutrition MUAC screening, WASH facility survey).
reference: IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Assessment Types Taxonomy; OCHA Assessment Registry

description: Number of children under 5 years of age in the assessed household. Key vulnerability indicator for nutrition (SAM/GAM screening), WASH (diarrhoea risk), and child protection programming. Required for UNICEF and WFP programme targeting.
reference: UNICEF Child Vulnerability Standards; SPHERE Handbook — Nutrition (Under-5 Focus); WFP Targeting Criteria

description: Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or household head prior to conducting the assessment. Mandatory per CHS Commitment 4 (Accountability to Affected Populations) and GDPR Article 6 for data processing lawfulness. Must be TRUE for the record to be validated.
reference: CHS Alliance — Commitment 4 Accountability; GDPR Article 6 Lawful Basis; HAP Standard

description: The form of informed consent obtained. 'verbal' is spoken consent recorded by enumerator; 'written' is signed consent form; 'proxy' is consent given by a guardian or community leader on behalf of a vulnerable individual (e.g., child, person with disability). Relevant for legal compliance and data protection audits.
reference: GDPR Article 7 — Conditions for Consent; CHS Alliance Data Protection Guidelines; ICRC Data Protection in Humanitarian Action

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the assessment was conducted (e.g., 'SOM', 'SSD', 'HTI'). Used for IATI reporting, donor country-level aggregation, and cross-country comparative analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IATI Standard v2.03 — Country Code

description: The date and time when this needs assessment record was first created in the data platform (Silver layer ingestion timestamp). Distinct from assessment_date (field event date). Used for data pipeline monitoring and audit trail compliance.
reference: FASB ASC 958 — Record Keeping; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 — Documentation Requirements

description: The primary method used to gather assessment data. 'face_to_face' is direct in-person interview; 'remote_phone' is telephone-based; 'fgd' is a Focus Group Discussion (FGD); 'kii' is a Key Informant Interview (KII); 'observation' is direct field observation without interview; 'secondary_data' is derived from existing records. Affects data quality weighting in vulnerability scoring.
reference: IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Data Collection Methodology; SPHERE Handbook

description: The displacement classification of the assessed individual or household at the time of assessment. 'idp' = Internally Displaced Person (IDP); 'refugee' = person with international protection status; 'returnee' = person returning to place of origin; 'host_community' = non-displaced community member hosting displaced persons. Critical for UNHCR/OCHA population movement tracking.
reference: UNHCR Refugee Definition (1951 Convention); OCHA IDP Tracking Matrix (DTM); Guiding Principles on Internal Displacement

description: Numeric score for the education sector assessment findings. Null if education was not included in sectors_assessed. Covers school access, enrollment barriers, learning environment safety, and teacher availability against SPHERE/INEE minimum standards.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Education; INEE Minimum Standards for Education in Emergencies; IASC Education Cluster

description: Free-text qualitative observations recorded by the field enumerator during the assessment. Captures contextual information not covered by structured questions (e.g., security constraints, access limitations, observed conditions). Used by supervisors during validation review.
reference: KoboToolbox — Note Question Type; CommCare — Case Note Field; CHS Alliance Data Quality Standards

description: Indicates whether the assessed household is headed by a female. Female-headed households are a standard vulnerability marker in humanitarian targeting criteria and are required for gender disaggregation in donor reports (USAID, DFID/FCDO, UN Women standards).
reference: USAID Gender Equality and Female Empowerment Policy; UN Women Humanitarian Action; IASC Gender Marker

description: Indicates whether the assessment identified elevated Gender-Based Violence (GBV) risk for the assessed individual or household. When TRUE, triggers referral to GBV case management pathway and PSS services. Classified as restricted PHI due to extreme sensitivity of GBV disclosure data.
reference: IASC GBV Guidelines; UNHCR GBVIMS Data Protection Protocol; SPHERE Handbook — Protection Principles

description: Estimated GPS accuracy in meters at the time of coordinate capture, as reported by the mobile device. KoboToolbox and CommCare record this automatically. Values above 30 meters may indicate unreliable location data and trigger data quality flags.
reference: KoboToolbox GPS Question Type — Accuracy Field; Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Data Quality Standards

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the assessment location captured by the field enumerator's device via KoboToolbox or CommCare GPS capture. Used for GIS mapping, cluster analysis, and geographic targeting in humanitarian response planning.
reference: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap — WGS84 Coordinate Standard; OCHA GIS Standards

description: Numeric score for the livelihoods sector assessment findings. Null if livelihoods was not included in sectors_assessed. Covers income sources, asset ownership, market access, and economic vulnerability indicators.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Livelihoods; IASC Livelihoods Cluster Assessment Framework; USAID BHA Livelihoods Assessment Standards

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the assessment location captured by the field enumerator's device. Used alongside latitude for GIS mapping, spatial clustering, and geographic targeting in humanitarian response planning.
reference: Humanitarian OpenStreetMap — WGS84 Coordinate Standard; OCHA GIS Standards

description: Mid-Upper Arm Circumference (MUAC) measurement in millimetres for children under 5 or pregnant/lactating women, used to screen for acute malnutrition. MUAC < 115mm indicates SAM; 115–125mm indicates MAM. Classified as PHI as it is a direct health measurement linked to an individual. Null if not applicable to assessment subject.
reference: WHO MUAC Screening Protocol; SPHERE Handbook — Nutrition; UNICEF MUAC Standards

description: Numeric score for the nutrition sector assessment findings. Null if nutrition was not included in sectors_assessed. Incorporates Global Acute Malnutrition (GAM) and Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) indicators where applicable. Feeds into DHIS2 nutrition indicator tracking.
reference: SPHERE Handbook — Nutrition Chapter; WHO/UNICEF GAM/SAM Thresholds; DHIS2 Nutrition Indicators

description: Composite numeric score representing the assessed subject's overall vulnerability level, calculated from sector-specific findings using the program's scoring methodology (e.g., 0–100 scale). Feeds into vulnerability_profile updates when the score materially changes vulnerability classification. This is a raw assessment output, not a derived KPI.
reference: IASC Needs Assessment Task Force — Vulnerability Scoring; UNHCR Vulnerability Assessment Framework

description: Number of household members with a physical, sensory, intellectual, or psychosocial disability. Required for disability-inclusive programming per IASC Guidelines on Inclusion of Persons with Disabilities in Humanitarian Action and CRPD compliance reporting.
reference: IASC Guidelines on Inclusion of Persons with Disabilities; UN CRPD; Washington Group Short Set on Disability

description: Numeric score for the protection sector assessment findings. Null if protection was not included in sectors_assessed. Covers GBV risk, child protection, housing land and property rights, and documentation status. Sensitive findings are handled per GBV information management protocols.
reference: IASC Protection Cluster — Assessment Standards; UNHCR GBV Information Management System (GBVIMS); SPHERE Handbook — Protection Principles

description: Indicates whether the assessment findings recommend referral of the beneficiary or household to one or more service providers or program interventions. When TRUE, a referral pathway is initiated in CommCare case management. Drives downstream case management workflow.
reference: CHS Alliance — Commitment 3 (Referral Pathways); CommCare Case Management Referral Module

description: The calendar date on which the beneficiary provided, withdrew, or refused consent. This is the principal real-world event date for the consent transaction, distinct from the record creation timestamp.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; FASB ASC 958

description: Version identifier of the consent form template used at the time of consent collection (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Enables tracking of which version of the consent language and terms the beneficiary agreed to, critical for compliance audits.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-3 language code representing the language in which consent was communicated and obtained. Ensures accountability that consent was given in the beneficiary's understood language (e.g., 'ar' for Arabic, 'fr' for French, 'so' for Somali). Critical for CHS accountability compliance.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; ISO 639-1 Language Codes; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: The method by which consent was obtained from the beneficiary. 'verbal' for spoken agreement (audio-recorded or witnessed); 'written' for signed paper form; 'digital' for electronic signature or mobile app confirmation; 'thumbprint' for biometric ink impression; 'proxy' for consent given by a guardian or authorized representative on behalf of a minor or incapacitated individual.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; GDPR Article 7; KoboToolbox Field Data Collection Standards

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference code assigned to this consent record, used in field operations, case referrals, and compliance reporting. Format: CNS-[COUNTRY]-[YEAR]-[SEQUENCE].
reference: KoboToolbox Field Data Collection Standards; IATI Transparency Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the consent record. 'given' indicates active informed consent; 'withdrawn' indicates the beneficiary has revoked consent; 'pending' indicates consent is awaited; 'expired' indicates the consent period has lapsed; 'refused' indicates explicit refusal.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Conditions for Consent); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Classification of the specific type of consent being recorded. Covers data processing consent (GDPR/privacy), photography and media use, inter-agency case referral, biometric enrollment, program participation, and research/survey participation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_processing|photography_media|case_referral|biometric_enrollment|program_participation|research_survey — promote to reference product]
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; GDPR Article 6; IATI Standard; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: The date and time when this consent record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, compliance reporting, and GDPR accountability obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) (Accountability Principle); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: The number of days for which the beneficiary's data may be retained under this consent, as agreed at the time of consent collection. Drives automated data retention and deletion workflows. Aligned with GDPR storage limitation principle.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(e) (Storage Limitation); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Description of the specific data elements, activities, or purposes covered by this consent record (e.g., 'collection and storage of household survey data', 'sharing of case data with UNHCR for referral'). Defines the boundaries of what the beneficiary has consented to.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(b) (Purpose Limitation); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; IATI Standard

description: Reference identifier or hash of the digital signature captured when consent_method is 'digital'. Links to the secure digital signature storage system. Not the signature itself — a reference token for audit trail purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 7; eIDAS Regulation (EU Electronic Signatures); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4

description: The date from which the consent becomes legally and operationally binding. May differ from consent_date if there is a processing delay or a future-dated consent arrangement.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4

description: The date on which the consent expires and must be renewed. Nullable for open-ended consents. Supports automated consent renewal workflows and compliance with data retention policies.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(e) (Storage Limitation); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; IATI Standard

description: File path, URL, or document management system reference to the scanned or digital copy of the signed consent form. Enables retrieval of the original consent document for audit and legal purposes.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; GDPR Article 7

description: Indicates whether the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies to this consent record based on the beneficiary's location, the organization's jurisdiction, or the data controller's domicile. True = GDPR applies; False = GDPR does not apply.
reference: GDPR Article 3 (Territorial Scope); GDPR Article 6 (Lawfulness of Processing)

description: Indicates whether a supervisor or quality assurance officer has verified that the consent was truly informed (i.e., the beneficiary understood the purpose, scope, and their rights before consenting). True = verified; False = pending verification.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: Indicates whether consent was given by a proxy (guardian, parent, or authorized representative) on behalf of a minor, elderly, or incapacitated beneficiary. True = proxy consent; False = direct consent from the beneficiary.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; GDPR Article 8 (Conditions for Child Consent)

description: Free-text field for additional contextual notes about the consent process, any special circumstances, beneficiary concerns raised, or follow-up actions required. Used by field staff and MEL officers for qualitative documentation.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

business_glossary_term: Photography and Media Use Permitted Flag
description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has consented to being photographed or filmed for organizational communications, donor reporting, or advocacy purposes. True = photography/media use permitted; False = not permitted.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard; GDPR Article 9

description: Full name of the proxy individual (guardian, parent, or authorized representative) who provided consent on behalf of the beneficiary. Populated only when is_proxy_consent is True.
reference: GDPR Article 8; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards

description: The relationship of the proxy consent giver to the beneficiary (e.g., parent, guardian, legal representative). Populated only when is_proxy_consent is True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent|guardian|spouse|sibling|community_leader|legal_representative|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; GDPR Article 8

description: Indicates whether the beneficiary has consented to their data being shared with third-party organizations (e.g., partner NGOs, UN agencies, government bodies). True = sharing permitted; False = sharing not permitted. Critical for inter-agency data sharing compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 6; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Free-text description of any specific restrictions or conditions placed by the beneficiary on data sharing (e.g., 'not to be shared with government authorities', 'only for health program purposes'). Populated when sharing_permitted is True but with conditions.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: The unique identifier of this consent record in the originating operational system (CommCare case ID, KoboToolbox submission UUID, Dynamics 365 record ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and source systems.
reference: IATI Standard; Internal Data Lineage and Governance Policy

description: The operational system of record from which this consent record was captured or ingested (e.g., CommCare for mobile case management, KoboToolbox for field surveys, Dynamics 365 for case management, Salesforce for constituent tracking, or manual for paper-based entry).
reference: IATI Standard; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this consent record was most recently modified. Tracks consent status changes, withdrawals, and verification updates for audit trail and data governance purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) (Accountability Principle); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The date on which a supervisor or quality assurance officer verified that the consent was properly informed and documented. Populated only when informed_consent_verified is True.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: The date on which the beneficiary formally withdrew their previously given consent. Populated only when consent_status is 'withdrawn'. Triggers downstream data deletion or anonymization workflows per GDPR right to erasure.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3) (Right to Withdraw Consent); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4

description: Free-text description of the reason provided by the beneficiary or their representative for withdrawing consent. Populated only when consent_status is 'withdrawn'. Used for accountability reporting and program improvement.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard

description: Full name of the field staff member or community witness who witnessed the consent process. Required for verbal and thumbprint consent methods to ensure accountability and legal validity.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) Commitment 4; HAP (Humanitarian Accountability Partnership) Standard; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards

description: Unique identifier for this enrollment record. Primary key for the enrollment association.
Foreign key to program.component. Identifies the program component in which the beneficiary is enrolled.
Foreign key linking to the beneficiary registrant participating in this component enrollment
Identifier of the NGO staff member or partner organization staff who processed this enrollment. Used for accountability, quality assurance, and workload tracking.

Percentage of scheduled component activities or sessions that the registrant attended. Calculated as (sessions_attended / sessions_scheduled) * 100. Used for engagement monitoring, outcome correlation analysis, and donor reporting on program participation quality. Explicitly identified in detection phase.

The date on which the registrant completed or exited this component enrollment. Null for active enrollments. Used for outcome tracking, retention analysis, and donor reporting on program completion rates. Explicitly identified in detection phase.

Indicates whether the registrant (or guardian) provided informed consent specifically for participation in this component. Some components (e.g., health screening, psychosocial support) require additional consent beyond general registration consent.
System timestamp when this enrollment record was created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

The date on which the registrant was formally enrolled into this program component. Used for cohort analysis, eligibility determination, and donor reporting on enrollment timelines. Explicitly identified in detection phase as enrollment_date.

Current lifecycle status of this enrollment record. Controls eligibility for component-specific services and activities. Values: enrolled (registered but not yet active), active (currently participating), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (successfully finished component), withdrawn (voluntarily left), exited (administratively removed). Explicitly identified in detection phase as enrollment_status.

Classification of the reason for enrollment termination or exit. Used for attrition analysis, program improvement, and donor reporting on retention and completion. Values include: completed (successfully finished), relocated (moved out of service area), deceased, ineligible (no longer meets criteria), voluntary-withdrawal, lost-to-follow-up, program-closure, other. Null for active enrollments. Explicitly identified in detection phase.
Geographic location (site, camp, community, district) where this enrollment was processed. Used for geographic disaggregation in MEL reporting and operational planning.

Source of referral into this component enrollment (self-referral, community health worker, partner organization, internal program referral, vulnerability assessment). Used for referral pathway analysis and partnership coordination.

The method or channel through which this component's services are delivered to the registrant. Distinguishes in-person group sessions, mobile outreach, remote/digital delivery, hybrid approaches, cash transfers, vouchers, or direct provision of goods/services. Critical for modality-disaggregated MEL reporting required by donors. Explicitly identified in detection phase.
System timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Unique surrogate identifier for each beneficiary-commodity entitlement record
Foreign key linking to the humanitarian commodity that the beneficiary is entitled to receive
Identifier of the donor funding this specific entitlement, if donor-restricted commodities are involved. Used for donor reporting and compliance with donor-imposed restrictions on commodity use.

Identifier of the humanitarian program or intervention under which this entitlement is granted (e.g., General Food Distribution, Blanket Supplementary Feeding, Emergency NFI Distribution). Links entitlement to funding source and program reporting.
Identifier of the system user who most recently modified this entitlement record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.
Foreign key linking to the beneficiary registrant who holds this commodity entitlement
Identifier of the system user or program officer who created this entitlement record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.
Timestamp when this entitlement record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and entitlement history tracking.

The date on which this entitlement expires or is scheduled to end. Null indicates ongoing entitlement subject to program continuation. Used for entitlement lifecycle management and program phase-out planning.
Current lifecycle status of this entitlement record. Controls whether the beneficiary is currently eligible to receive this commodity in distribution operations.
The frequency at which the beneficiary is entitled to receive this commodity (e.g., monthly food ration, one-time NFI kit, weekly fresh food distribution). Drives distribution cycle planning.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this entitlement record. Used for change tracking and synchronization.

The quantity of the commodity that the beneficiary is entitled to receive per distribution cycle, expressed in the commodity's standard unit of measure. Used for ration calculation and distribution planning.

Free-text description of any special dietary requirements, restrictions, or substitutions applicable to this beneficiary-commodity entitlement (e.g., halal, kosher, allergen-free, therapeutic food for MAM/SAM cases). Used for commodity substitution and specialized ration planning.
The date from which this entitlement becomes active and the beneficiary is eligible to receive the commodity. Used for entitlement verification and distribution eligibility checks.

Multiplier or adjustment factor applied to the base entitlement quantity based on the beneficiary's vulnerability category, protection status, or special needs (e.g., 1.5x for pregnant/lactating women, 2.0x for severely malnourished children). Applied during ration calculation.
Primary key for household_volunteer_assignment
Unique system-generated identifier for the household-volunteer assignment record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to the household receiving volunteer services
Foreign key linking to the volunteer assigned to provide services
Calendar date when the volunteer was formally assigned to this household for service delivery. Marks the beginning of the assignment relationship.
Calendar date when the assignment was formally ended or closed. Null for active assignments. Used for historical tracking and volunteer engagement reporting.

Current lifecycle state of the volunteer-household assignment. Active indicates ongoing service delivery, Suspended indicates temporary pause, Completed indicates assignment ended successfully, Transferred indicates household reassigned to another volunteer.
Classification of the service delivery type for this assignment. Defines the primary program area or service category the volunteer provides to the household.
Geographic zone, sector, or catchment area identifier for this assignment. Used to group households by volunteer service area for workload balancing and geographic coverage planning.
Calendar date of the most recent visit by this volunteer to this household. Updated after each completed visit for monitoring and follow-up scheduling.
Calendar date of the next scheduled visit by this volunteer to this household. Used for volunteer scheduling and workload planning.
Free-text field for capturing assignment-specific notes, special instructions, household preferences, or contextual information relevant to service delivery.
Expected frequency of volunteer visits to the household based on program requirements and household needs assessment.
Unique identifier for the referral transaction. Primary key for the referral data product.
Identifier of the case management record associated with this referral, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to communication.constituent_message. Business justification: Referral coordination requires messaging beneficiaries with appointment details, location instructions, and follow-up reminders. Tracking which message was sent for which referral enables referral com

Foreign key linking to compliance.corrective_action_plan. Business justification: When audits identify referral pathway failures (delays, incomplete follow-up, partner coordination gaps), corrective action plans document remediation. Links specific referrals to quality improvement
Identifier of the specific program within the referring organization that is making the referral.

Foreign key linking to field.project_site. Business justification: Referrals specify destination field facilities for service delivery. Core to protection and health referral pathway management, inter-agency coordination, and service accessibility analysis. Role-pref
Identifier of the organization or service provider that is receiving and expected to act on the referral.
Identifier of the staff member at the receiving organization who is assigned to handle this referral.
Identifier of the organization or partner that is initiating and submitting the referral.
Identifier of the staff member or case worker who initiated the referral on behalf of the beneficiary.

Foreign key linking to volunteer.volunteer. Business justification: Community volunteers identify and refer beneficiaries to specialized services (health, protection, legal aid). Critical for community-based protection, health outreach, and early identification progra
Identifier of the beneficiary being referred to another service provider or program.
Date when the receiving organization formally accepted the referral and committed to providing services.
Numeric rating provided by the beneficiary indicating their satisfaction with the referral process and services received, typically on a scale of 1-5.
Explanation for why the referral was cancelled before completion, including beneficiary circumstances or organizational constraints.
Date when the referral was marked as completed, indicating that all required services have been delivered and documented.
Classification of the sensitivity of the referral case, particularly important for Gender-Based Violence (GBV) and protection cases requiring special handling.
Date when informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary for the referral and information sharing.
Indicates whether informed consent was obtained from the beneficiary or their guardian before making the referral, in compliance with protection and data privacy standards.
Timestamp when this referral record was first created in the system.
Name of the operational system from which this referral record originated, such as CommCare, KoboToolbox, or Microsoft Dynamics 365.
Explanation provided when a referral is declined by the receiving organization or rejected by the beneficiary.
Target date by which the receiving organization is expected to acknowledge or respond to the referral.
Indicates whether feedback was received from the beneficiary regarding their satisfaction with the referral process and services received.
Indicates whether the scheduled follow-up with the beneficiary or receiving organization has been completed.
Scheduled date for follow-up contact with the beneficiary or receiving organization to verify service delivery and outcome.
Indicates whether this referral is related to a Gender-Based Violence (GBV) case, requiring adherence to specialized GBV referral pathways and confidentiality protocols.
Timestamp when this referral record was last updated or modified in the system.
Additional free-text notes, observations, or context about the referral that may be relevant for case management and coordination.
Detailed description of the outcome of the referral, including services provided, beneficiary response, and any follow-up actions required.
Standardized classification of the referral outcome for reporting and analysis purposes.
Urgency classification of the referral indicating how quickly the beneficiary needs to receive services.
reference: Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) Emergency Response Standards

description: Indicates whether the referral involves protection concerns such as child protection, trafficking, exploitation, or other safeguarding issues.
Detailed explanation of why the beneficiary is being referred, including specific needs, conditions, or circumstances that necessitate the referral.
Name of the organization or service provider receiving the referral, for tracking and reporting purposes.
Specific type of service or program that the beneficiary is being referred to at the receiving organization.
reference: Sphere Humanitarian Charter and Minimum Standards

description: Full name of the staff member at the receiving organization who accepted or is processing the referral.

Primary service category or sector for which the beneficiary is being referred. Aligned with humanitarian cluster system and Sphere standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|protection|shelter|wash|nutrition|education|livelihoods|psychosocial|legal|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Sphere Humanitarian Charter and Minimum Standards

description: The date when the referral was initiated and submitted to the receiving organization or service provider.
Human-readable business identifier for the referral, used for tracking and communication across organizations.
Current lifecycle status of the referral indicating whether it has been accepted, is being processed, completed, or declined by the receiving organization.

Classification of the referral based on organizational scope and urgency. Internal referrals are within the same organization, external are to partner organizations, emergency referrals require immediate action.
Phone number or email address of the referring staff member for coordination and follow-up communication.
Full name of the staff member who initiated the referral, for contact and follow-up purposes.
Date when the service was actually delivered to the beneficiary by the receiving organization.
Unique identifier for each volunteer-beneficiary service assignment record. Primary key for the association.
Foreign key linking to the beneficiary registrant receiving services from the volunteer
Identifier of the program coordinator or case manager who made this volunteer-beneficiary assignment.
Foreign key linking to the volunteer providing services to the beneficiary
The date when this volunteer was formally assigned to this beneficiary by program coordination staff.
Priority level of this beneficiary within the volunteer's caseload. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
The date when the volunteer stopped or is scheduled to stop providing services to this beneficiary. Null indicates ongoing service. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
How frequently the volunteer provides services to this beneficiary. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
The location where services are delivered (community center, home visit, health post, etc.). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
Free-text notes about this specific service assignment, including special considerations, beneficiary preferences, or coordination details.
Current status of the service delivery relationship between this volunteer and beneficiary. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
The type of service the volunteer is providing to this beneficiary. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
The date when the volunteer began providing services to this beneficiary. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
The level of supervision required for this volunteer-beneficiary service relationship. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning as relationship data.
Unique identifier for the advocacy campaign. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Advocacy campaigns must comply with donor restrictions (political activity limits, lobbying prohibitions, earmarking constraints). Real process: campaign compliance review validates campaign against d

Foreign key linking to communication.media_contact. Business justification: advocacy_campaign currently duplicates media contact details (media_contact_name, media_contact_email, media_contact_phone). The media_contact master product exists with PK media_contact_id. This FK n

Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Advocacy campaigns are typically owned and budgeted at organizational unit level (country office, regional department) for accountability, budget tracking, and performance reporting. Nonprofit domain

Foreign key linking to communication.communication_plan. Business justification: Advocacy campaigns are typically executed as part of broader communication plans. This link enables tracking campaign deliverables against plan objectives, budget allocation from plan to campaign, and
The actual number of meaningful engagements (clicks, shares, responses, sign-ups) generated by the campaign.
The actual fundraising revenue generated by the campaign to date, tracked through gift processing and donor management systems.
The actual number of individuals or households reached or engaged by the campaign, measured through analytics and tracking systems.
Total amount actually spent on the campaign to date, tracked for budget versus actual (BvA) reporting and financial stewardship.
The date when the campaign was formally approved by leadership or compliance team to proceed.
Total budget allocated to the campaign for all activities, including creative development, media buys, event costs, and staff time.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget allocated amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The specific action the campaign asks constituents to take, such as donate, sign a petition, attend an event, share content, or contact a legislator.
Short alphanumeric code used for internal tracking, reporting, and integration with donor management and financial systems.
Detailed narrative description of the campaign purpose, strategy, key messages, and expected impact.
Primary objective or intended outcome of the campaign, such as raising awareness for a specific issue, mobilizing advocacy actions, generating donations, or influencing policy change.
The official name of the advocacy, awareness, or fundraising campaign as recognized by the communications team and stakeholders.
Name of the staff member or team responsible for managing and executing the campaign.

Current lifecycle status of the campaign: planned (in design phase), active (currently running), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (finished and closed), cancelled (terminated before completion), or archived (historical record).

Classification of the campaign delivery method: digital (social media, email, web), direct mail (postal outreach), event-based (conferences, rallies), media (TV, radio, press), grassroots (community organizing), or hybrid (multi-channel).
The date when the campaign was formally closed out, including final reporting, financial reconciliation, and archival of materials.

Status of internal compliance and legal review for campaign messaging and materials: pending (under review), approved (cleared for launch), rejected (requires revision), or not_required (no review needed).
The date and time when this campaign record was first created in the system.
The planned or actual date when the campaign concludes. Null for ongoing campaigns without a defined end.
Geographic scope or target region for the campaign, such as global, regional (e.g., Sub-Saharan Africa), national, or local (e.g., specific city or community).
The primary social media hashtag used to promote and track the campaign on digital platforms.
Additional social media hashtags used in the campaign. Comma-separated list.
Indicates whether the campaign is funded by donor-restricted grants or contributions that limit how funds can be used. True if restricted, False if unrestricted.
reference: Financial Accounting Standards Board ASC 958 (Not-for-Profit Entities)

description: The primary language in which campaign materials and messaging are delivered (e.g., English, Spanish, French, Arabic).
Additional languages in which campaign materials are available, if applicable. Comma-separated list.
The date and time when this campaign record was last updated or modified.
The date when the campaign was officially launched to the public or target audience. May differ from start_date if there was a soft launch or pilot phase.

Comma-separated list of partner organizations, Civil Society Organizations (CSO), Community-Based Organizations (CBO), or International Non-Governmental Organizations (INGO) collaborating on the campaign.
Comma-separated list or description of the primary communication channels used in the campaign (e.g., social media, email, direct mail, events, media relations).
Comma-separated list of United Nations Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers or names that this campaign supports or aligns with (e.g., SDG 1: No Poverty, SDG 3: Good Health and Well-being).
The date when the campaign officially begins or launched to the public.
Description of the primary audience or constituent segment the campaign is designed to reach, such as major donors, youth advocates, policy makers, or general public.
The planned number of meaningful engagements (clicks, shares, responses, sign-ups) the campaign aims to generate.
The fundraising revenue goal for the campaign, if applicable. Null for non-fundraising campaigns.
The planned number of individuals or households the campaign aims to reach or engage.
The overarching theme or narrative of the campaign, such as humanitarian response, climate action, gender equality, or education access.
The URL of the dedicated campaign landing page or microsite where constituents can learn more and take action.

Indicates whether the media contact responded to the campaign outreach (true if response received, false if no response)
Unique identifier for the campaign touchpoint record. Primary key.

Identifier of the advocacy or fundraising campaign associated with this touchpoint. Links to the campaign master record.
Identifier of the constituent (donor, volunteer, advocate, or beneficiary) who interacted with the campaign. Links to the constituent master record in Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud.

description: The communication channel through which the constituent interacted with the campaign. Supports multi-channel attribution analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|web|mobile_app|social_media|sms|direct_mail|phone|in_person — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The allocated marketing cost associated with generating this specific touchpoint, expressed in US Dollars. Used for campaign Return on Investment (ROI) and cost-effectiveness analysis.

A calculated score representing the depth and quality of constituent engagement with this touchpoint. Higher scores indicate more meaningful interactions. Used for constituent segmentation and predictive modeling.

The verbatim feedback or comment provided by the constituent during this touchpoint interaction. Supports qualitative analysis and constituent sentiment tracking.
The geographic location (city, region, or country) from which the constituent engaged with the campaign. Supports geographic segmentation and localized campaign strategies.

description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the language in which the campaign communication was delivered. Supports multilingual campaign management.
The subject line or headline of the communication that generated this touchpoint (e.g., email subject, social media post headline). Used for content performance analysis.
The specific variant or version of the campaign message (e.g., 'Version A', 'Version B', 'Control Group'). Supports A/B testing and multivariate campaign analysis.

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a specific partner organization's participation in a specific advocacy campaign.
