The current approval status of the partner organization's participation in this campaign. Pending indicates invitation sent but not yet confirmed; approved indicates partner has confirmed participation; declined indicates partner declined the invitation; withdrawn indicates partner initially approved but later withdrew. This tracks the lifecycle of each participation relationship.

The type of contribution the partner organization provides to this specific campaign. Examples include advocacy (policy influence), content (materials and messaging), funding (financial support), field implementation (on-ground activities), technical expertise (specialized knowledge), media amplification (press and social reach), or coalition building (network mobilization). A partner may have different contribution types across different campaigns.
System timestamp when this participation record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

The date when the partner organization's active participation in this campaign ended or is planned to end. May differ from the campaign end date if the partner's involvement is time-limited. Null for ongoing participation. Used for tracking partner engagement duration and post-campaign evaluation.

The date when the partner organization's active participation in this campaign began. May differ from the campaign start date if the partner joined mid-campaign. Used for tracking partner engagement duration and coordination timelines.

The specific role the partner organization plays in this campaign. Distinguishes between lead partners (primary coordinators), supporting partners (secondary contributors), implementing partners (field execution), technical partners (subject matter expertise), financial partners (funding contributors), and media partners (amplification and outreach). This attribute exists only in the context of a specific partner-campaign relationship.
System timestamp when this participation record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

The level of public visibility and attribution the partner organization receives for their participation in this campaign. Public means prominently featured in campaign materials; acknowledged means listed as a partner but not prominently featured; confidential means partner involvement is tracked internally but not publicly disclosed; anonymous means partner requested no attribution. Visibility level varies by partner preference and campaign context.

description: Calendar date on which all due diligence checks were completed and a final outcome was recorded. Null if the process is still in progress.

description: Free-text description of any conditions, restrictions, or corrective actions attached to an approved-with-conditions outcome. Documents required remediation steps and timelines before full approval is granted.

description: Full descriptive name of the accreditation, certification, or standard (e.g., 'Core Humanitarian Standard Certification', 'ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management', 'Sphere Humanitarian Standards Compliance', 'HAP Membership', 'National NGO Registration – Kenya'). description: Current lifecycle status of the accreditation record. 'active' indicates the accreditation is valid and in force; 'expired' indicates the validity period has lapsed; 'suspended' indicates temporary withdrawal by the accrediting body; 'pending_renewal' indicates renewal application submitted; 'withdrawn' indicates permanent revocation; 'under_review' indicates the accrediting body is conducting a review.

description: Broad category of the accreditation record. description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the accrediting body is domiciled or where the registration is issued (e.g., 'CHE' for Switzerland-based CHS Alliance, 'GBR' for UK Charity Commission). Supports geographic compliance analysis.

description: Full name of the organization or authority that issued the accreditation or certification (e.g., 'CHS Alliance', 'Sphere Association', 'Bureau Veritas', 'Kenya NGO Coordination Board', 'ISO Certification Body'). Critical for verification and audit trail purposes.

description: Most recently reported annual organizational budget of the partner in US Dollars. Used for financial capacity assessment, sub-award sizing decisions, and due diligence under OMB Uniform Guidance. Sourced from partner financial disclosures or audited accounts.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; FASB ASC 958; USAID Partner Capacity Assessment

description: Full name of the individual legally authorized to sign agreements (MoUs, LoAs, SoWs, sub-award agreements) on behalf of the partner organization. Required for agreement execution and legal compliance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; USAID Standard Provisions

description: Official title of the authorized signatory at the partner organization (e.g., Executive Director, CEO, Country Representative). Validates signing authority for sub-award and partnership agreements.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID Standard Provisions

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the partner organization's primary bank account is held. Used for international wire transfer compliance and OFAC/sanctions screening.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; OFAC Sanctions Regulations; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.305

description: Name of the primary banking institution used by the partner organization for receiving sub-grant disbursements. Required for financial due diligence and sub-award payment processing in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.305; USAID Standard Provisions; FASB ASC 958

description: Date on which the most recent formal partner capacity assessment was conducted. Used to determine whether a reassessment is due and to track the currency of due diligence records.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; USAID Partner Capacity Assessment Framework

description: Numeric score (typically 0–100) resulting from the most recent formal partner capacity assessment covering governance, financial management, HR, and program delivery. Used to determine sub-award eligibility, required oversight level, and capacity-strengthening needs under OMB Uniform Guidance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; USAID Partner Capacity Assessment; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS)

description: Indicates whether the partner organization holds a valid Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS) certification. CHS certification demonstrates commitment to accountability, quality, and improvement in humanitarian response.

Comma-separated list of OCHA humanitarian cluster(s) in which the partner organization holds active membership or co-lead roles (e.g., WASH, Health, Shelter, Food Security, Protection). Used for coordination mapping and IASC working group engagement tracking.
reference: OCHA Cluster Coordination System; Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC)

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the partner organization has active field operations. Distinct from country of registration. Used for geographic coverage analysis and OCHA 3W (Who does What Where) reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; IATI Activity Standard v2.03; OCHA 3W Reporting

description: Date on which the current due diligence clearance expires and a new assessment must be completed before the partner can enter into new sub-award or partnership agreements.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; USAID Partner Vetting System

description: Current status of the partner organization's due diligence vetting process. 'Expired' indicates that a previously completed assessment has passed its validity period and renewal is required before new agreements can be executed.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; USAID Partner Vetting System; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS)

description: Email address of the partner organization's designated financial officer or finance focal point. Used for Budget versus Actual (BvA) reporting, financial documentation requests, and sub-grant financial management communications.
reference: GDPR Article 4; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; FASB ASC 958

description: Calendar year in which the partner organization was founded or established. Used to assess organizational maturity, track record, and eligibility for capacity-strengthening programs.
reference: IATI Organisation Standard v2.03; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Full mailing address of the partner organization's global headquarters including street, city, postal code, and country. Used for official correspondence, legal notices, and sub-award agreement documentation.

description: City where the partner organization's global headquarters is located. Used for correspondence, coordination meetings, and geographic reporting.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the partner organization's global headquarters is located. Used for geographic analysis, donor reporting, and OCHA cluster coordination mapping.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; IATI Organisation Standard v2.03

business_glossary_term: International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) Organization Identifier

description: Official mission statement of the partner organization describing its core purpose and mandate. Used for alignment assessment with Ngo's Theory of Change (ToC) and program design.

description: Commonly used acronym or short name for the partner organization (e.g., CARE, IRC, MSF). Widely used in SitReps, cluster coordination, and OCHA reporting to identify partners concisely.

description: Full legal registered name of the partner organization as it appears on official registration documents. Used as the primary human-readable identity label for the partner across all systems including Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Salesforce Nonprofit Cloud.
reference: IATI Organisation Standard v2.03; Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS)

description: Categorical classification of the partner organization type. Distinguishes between International Non-Governmental Organizations (INGOs), Civil Society Organizations (CSOs), Community-Based Organizations (CBOs), UN agencies, government bodies, consortia, private sector entities, and academic institutions. Drives eligibility rules for sub-award agreements and due diligence requirements. description: Official public website URL of the partner organization. Used for due diligence verification, public profile validation, and IATI transparency reporting.

'pending_vetting' indicates due diligence is in progress; 'blacklisted' indicates the partner is barred from engagement per donor or regulatory requirements.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.332; USAID Partner Vetting System

description: ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the partner organization's preferred language for official communications, reporting, and agreement documentation (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'ar', 'es').
Official email address of the primary focal point at the partner organization. Used for formal communications, agreement notifications, and reporting requests.

Full name of the primary focal point or authorized representative at the partner organization. This is the principal contact for partnership coordination, agreement negotiations, and official communications.
Direct phone number (including country code) for the primary focal point at the partner organization. Used for urgent field coordination and emergency communications.

Job title or designation of the primary focal point at the partner organization (e.g., Country Director, Partnership Manager, Executive Director). Used to assess seniority and authority level for agreement signing.
Timestamp indicating when the partner organization record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Timestamp indicating when the partner organization record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.

description: Date on which the most recent sanctions screening was completed for the partner organization. Used to determine whether re-screening is required before new agreements or disbursements.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Regulations; USAID Partner Vetting System; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Indicates whether the partner organization has been screened against applicable sanctions lists (e.g., OFAC SDN List, UN Consolidated Sanctions List, EU Sanctions List) as part of the due diligence process. Required for USAID and US Government-funded programs.
reference: OFAC Sanctions Regulations; UN Security Council Sanctions; USAID Partner Vetting System; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Comma-separated list of UN Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) numbers that the partner organization's mandate aligns with (e.g., '1,2,3,6'). Used for strategic alignment reporting and donor narrative reporting.
reference: UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) Framework; IATI Activity Standard v2.03

description: Total number of paid staff (national and international) employed by the partner organization at the time of last capacity assessment. Used as an indicator of organizational capacity for program delivery and sub-award management.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS); USAID Partner Capacity Assessment Framework

description: Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN in the US, VAT number in the EU) for the partner organization. Required for financial transactions, sub-award agreements, and compliance with NICRA (Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement) reporting.
reference: IRS 501(c)(3) Regulations (US); OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 §200.330; FASB ASC 958

description: Comma-separated list of primary thematic areas in which the partner organization operates (e.g., WASH, GBV, PSS, Health, Education, Livelihoods, NFI Distribution). Used for partner matching in program design and cluster coordination with OCHA.
reference: OCHA Cluster System; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; SDG Framework

description: Total number of active volunteers engaged by the partner organization. Reflects community mobilization capacity and is used in operational capacity assessments for field program delivery.
reference: Core Humanitarian Standard (CHS); IATI Organisation Standard v2.03

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each risk entry in the partner risk register. Primary key for this data product.

Foreign key linking to partnership.due_diligence_record. Business justification: partner_risk_register stores capacity_assessment_score and capacity_assessment_date but has no FK to due_diligence_record. Risks are identified through due diligence processes. Adding due_diligence_re
Reference to the partner organization for which this risk entry is recorded. Links to the partner master record.
reference: Microsoft Dynamics 365 for Nonprofits — Partner/Account Record

description: Reference to the staff member record of the designated risk owner responsible for monitoring and managing this risk. Enables automated escalation workflows and accountability tracking.
reference: Workday HCM — Worker Record; CHS Alliance RACI Framework

description: Reference to the specific sub-award agreement under which this risk is being assessed, if applicable. Links to the sub-award management record.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 Subpart F — Audit Requirements for Sub-Awards

description: Calculated product of likelihood_rating multiplied by impact_rating (range 1–25), yielding the overall risk severity score used to prioritise mitigation actions and sub-award oversight decisions. Stored as a business-recorded value from the risk assessment process, not a real-time derived metric.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303 Risk Scoring Methodology

description: Timestamp when this risk register entry was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for data governance and compliance reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 958; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Indicates whether the risk event or materialisation requires formal notification to the donor per grant agreement terms (True). Relevant for USAID, DFID/FCDO, CERF, and other institutional donors with mandatory incident reporting clauses.
reference: USAID ADS 303; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; DFID Smart Rules — Incident Reporting

description: Date on which the donor was formally notified of this risk or incident. Null if notification has not yet occurred or is not required. Used for compliance tracking against donor agreement notification timelines.
reference: USAID ADS 303; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; DFID Smart Rules

description: Level of due diligence applied to this partner in relation to this risk: standard (routine checks), enhanced (additional scrutiny for high-risk partners), simplified (low-risk, minimal checks). Aligns with donor-mandated partner vetting requirements.
reference: USAID ADS 303 — Partner Vetting; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; DFID Smart Rules

description: Name or role of the individual, committee, or external body to whom this risk was escalated (e.g., 'Country Director', 'Board Risk Committee', 'USAID Agreement Officer'). Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.
reference: CHS Alliance — Core Humanitarian Standard; USAID ADS 303; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Date on which this risk was formally escalated to senior management, the board, or the donor. Null if escalation has not occurred. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.
reference: CHS Alliance — Core Humanitarian Standard; USAID ADS 303

description: Indicates whether this risk has been flagged for escalation to senior management, the board, or the donor (True). Set automatically when risk_level is 'critical' or when safeguarding_risk_flag is True.
reference: CHS Alliance — Core Humanitarian Standard; USAID ADS 303; OCHA Risk Escalation Protocols

description: Indicates whether this risk entry has been classified as a fiduciary risk (True) involving financial management, fund accountability, or procurement integrity concerns. Triggers enhanced financial monitoring protocols.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 Subpart F; USAID ADS 303; FASB ASC 958

description: Estimated maximum financial exposure in US Dollars associated with this risk, representing the value of funds at risk if the risk materialises (e.g., sub-award value at risk of misappropriation). Used in fiduciary risk prioritisation and BvA oversight.
reference: FASB ASC 958 — Not-for-Profit Entities; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200; USAID ADS 303

description: Numeric score (1–5) representing the severity of consequences if the risk materialises: 1=Negligible, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Major, 5=Catastrophic. Assessed across financial, programmatic, reputational, and safeguarding dimensions.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; SPHERE Humanitarian Standards; USAID ADS 303

description: Impact rating (1–5) assessed BEFORE any mitigation controls are applied. Together with inherent_likelihood_rating, establishes the gross risk score prior to control application.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303

description: Likelihood rating (1–5) assessed BEFORE any mitigation controls are applied. Captures the gross/inherent risk level to benchmark the effectiveness of mitigation measures.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303

description: The date on which this risk entry was most recently reviewed and updated by the risk owner or oversight team. Used to flag stale entries requiring re-assessment.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331

description: Numeric score (1–5) representing the probability that the risk will materialise: 1=Rare, 2=Unlikely, 3=Possible, 4=Likely, 5=Almost Certain. Used in composite risk scoring per standard NGO risk matrices.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303 Risk Rating Scale

description: Narrative description of the specific controls, actions, and safeguards implemented or planned to reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk. May include capacity-building, enhanced financial monitoring, spot-checks, or escalation protocols.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303

description: Current implementation status of the mitigation measures: planned (not yet started), in_progress (underway), completed (fully implemented), overdue (past target date without completion).
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; IASC Risk Management Policy

description: Frequency at which this risk entry is actively monitored and reviewed: weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi_annual, annual, or ad_hoc. Drives scheduling of oversight activities and MEL review cycles.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303; IATI Standard

description: Scheduled date for the next formal review of this risk entry. Drives automated reminders and compliance with periodic risk review obligations under donor agreements.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Free-text field for additional context, observations, or supplementary information related to this risk entry that does not fit into structured fields. May include references to related incident reports, audit findings, or SitRep entries.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs

description: Description of the specific oversight mechanism applied to manage this risk (e.g., 'Monthly financial spot-checks', 'Quarterly field monitoring visits', 'Third-party audit', 'PDM surveys'). Documents the control environment for donor compliance.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331; USAID ADS 303; CHS Alliance

description: Assessment of whether this risk falls within the organisation's defined risk appetite: within_appetite (acceptable), at_tolerance (borderline, requires monitoring), exceeds_appetite (unacceptable, requires immediate action or sub-award suspension).
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; IASC Risk Management Policy; Charity Commission (UK) Risk Management Guidance

description: High-level classification of the risk type: fiduciary (financial mismanagement, fraud), programmatic (delivery failure, quality gaps), reputational (media, political exposure), safeguarding (protection of beneficiaries and staff), or compliance (regulatory, donor-condition breaches).
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303 Partner Risk Assessment

description: The date on which this risk was formally closed, either because it was fully mitigated, the sub-award ended, or the risk was deemed no longer applicable. Null if the risk remains open.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; IATI Standard

description: Detailed narrative describing the nature of the risk, its potential causes, and the context in which it may materialise. Supports due diligence documentation and donor compliance reporting.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.331 — Requirements for Pass-Through Entities; USAID ADS 303

description: The date on which this risk was first formally identified and entered into the risk register. Marks the start of the risk lifecycle and is used for ageing analysis.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303

description: Qualitative risk band derived from the composite risk score: low (1–4), medium (5–9), high (10–16), critical (17–25). Used for traffic-light reporting in MEL dashboards, donor SitReps, and sub-award oversight decisions.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; OCHA Risk Management Framework

description: Date on which the risk was confirmed to have materialised into an actual incident or adverse event. Null if the risk has not materialised. Used for incident timeline analysis and donor reporting.
reference: CHS Alliance — Core Humanitarian Standard; USAID ADS 303

description: Indicates whether the identified risk has actually materialised into an incident or adverse event (True). When True, triggers incident management workflows and may require sub-award suspension or termination review.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; USAID ADS 303; OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200

description: Name or role title of the individual within the NGO responsible for monitoring and managing this risk entry. Typically a Program Manager, Grants Manager, or Country Director.
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; RACI Framework

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to this risk entry for cross-system reference and reporting (e.g., RR-FISC-2024-0012). Used in donor reports, audit trails, and compliance filings.
reference: IATI Standard — Organisation and Activity Reporting; CHS Alliance Risk Management Framework

description: Current lifecycle state of the risk entry: open (identified, not yet addressed), under_review (being assessed), mitigated (controls applied), escalated (elevated to senior management or donor), closed (resolved or no longer applicable).
reference: CHS Alliance — Risk Management for NGOs; IASC Risk Management Policy

The monetary value of the cost being allocated from the source to the target. Represents the actual dollar amount distributed in this allocation transaction.

The quantitative basis or driver used to calculate the allocation distribution, such as headcount, square footage, direct costs, full-time equivalents (FTE), revenue, beneficiary count, transaction volume, or time spent. The quantitative measure of the allocation basis used in the calculation (e.g., number of employees, square feet, total direct costs). Used in conjunction with allocation_rate to compute allocated_amount.
The unit of measure for the allocation basis quantity (e.g., employees, square_feet, USD, FTE, hours).
The business date on which the cost allocation was executed and recorded in the general ledger.
Externally-known unique document number for this cost allocation transaction, used for audit trail and reference in financial systems.

The methodology used to distribute costs across grants, programs, or cost centers. Common methods include direct assignment, proportional distribution, NICRA-based rates, step-down allocation, activity-based costing, headcount-based, or square footage-based. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|proportional|nicra_based|step_down|activity_based|headcount|square_footage — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Free-text notes or comments providing additional context, justification, or documentation for this cost allocation transaction.
The rate or percentage applied to the allocation basis to calculate the allocated amount. For NICRA-based allocations, this represents the negotiated indirect cost rate.

Current lifecycle status of the cost allocation transaction: draft (being prepared), pending approval (awaiting review), approved (authorized), posted (recorded in GL), reversed (cancelled), or rejected (not approved).
The date on which this cost allocation was approved by the authorized financial manager or controller.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this cost allocation transaction.
Reference identifier linking this cost allocation to supporting documentation, audit logs, or source system transaction records for compliance and audit purposes.
Indicator of whether this cost allocation meets OMB Uniform Guidance and donor compliance requirements: compliant, non-compliant, under review, or exempt.
Classification of the cost being allocated: direct program costs, indirect costs, facilities and administration (F&A), shared services, or overhead.
The name or identifier of the cost pool from which costs are being allocated. Cost pools aggregate similar indirect costs for distribution.
The date and time when this cost allocation record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Boolean flag indicating whether this allocation represents Facilities and Administration (F&A) costs, also known as indirect costs, that are recoverable under federal grants.
Boolean flag indicating whether the target fund is a restricted fund with donor-imposed limitations on the use of resources.
The date and time when this cost allocation record was last modified or updated in the system.
The date on which this cost allocation was posted to the general ledger and became part of the official financial records.
The date on which this cost allocation was reversed or cancelled, if applicable. Null if the allocation has not been reversed.
Explanation for why this cost allocation was reversed or cancelled, if applicable.
Unique identifier for this fund-requirement compliance tracking record. Primary key.
Reference to the staff member assigned responsibility for ensuring this fund's compliance with this specific requirement. May differ from the general requirement owner.
Foreign key to donor_requirement. Identifies which donor requirement applies to this fund.
Foreign key to finance_fund. Identifies which fund this compliance tracking applies to.

business_glossary_term: Fund Compliance Tracking - Finance Fund Id
description: Foreign key linking to the fund being tracked for compliance

Current status of this fund's compliance with this specific requirement. Tracks progress from initiation through approval or waiver. Distinct from the donor_requirement.compliance_status which may aggregate across multiple funds.
Timestamp when this fund-requirement compliance tracking record was created in the system.
Timestamp when this compliance tracking record was last updated.
The most recent date on which compliance with this requirement was verified for this fund. Used for audit trail and to determine when next verification is due.
Free-text notes specific to this fund's compliance with this requirement, including fund-specific challenges, accommodations, or context.
The date on which this donor requirement ceases to apply to this specific fund. May differ from fund effective_end_date if requirement is waived or modified.
The date from which this donor requirement becomes applicable to this specific fund. May differ from the fund effective_start_date if requirement is added mid-grant.
Date on which the donor made a decision (granted or denied) on the waiver request for this fund-requirement combination.
Date on which a waiver was formally requested for this fund-requirement combination.
Status of any waiver request for this requirement as it applies to this specific fund. A requirement may be waived for one fund but not others.
Unique identifier for the grant budget record. Primary key for the grant budget entity in Unit4 ERP.
Reference to the parent grant award for which this budget was approved. Links to the grant award master record.

Foreign key linking to field.country_office. Business justification: Grant budgets are managed at country office level. Finance needs to track grant budgets by country office for country-level grant management and reporting.

Foreign key linking to compliance.donor_requirement. Business justification: Grant budgets must satisfy donor-specific compliance requirements (budget format, cost categories, indirect cost limitations). Award managers link budgets to requirements for compliance verification d

Classification of the budget by funding purpose and restriction type. Aligns with FASB ASC 958 net asset classification requirements.
Date when the grant budget was officially closed out after all financial reporting and reconciliation requirements were completed.
Unique alphanumeric code assigned to this grant budget in Unit4 ERP for tracking and reporting purposes. Used in Chart of Accounts (CoA) mapping and General Ledger (GL) integration.
Descriptive name of the grant budget, typically reflecting the program or project name and donor.
Detailed narrative justification and explanation of the budget line items, cost assumptions, and allocation methodology submitted to the donor.
Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to budget management, donor restrictions, or compliance requirements.
End date of the budget period. Expenditures after this date may require prior approval or budget modification.
Start date of the budget period during which approved funds may be obligated and expended.

Current lifecycle status of the grant budget. Tracks progression from draft through approval to active execution and eventual closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|active|amended|closed|suspended — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date when the grant budget was submitted to the donor for review and approval.
Version number of the budget document. Increments with each approved modification or amendment to maintain audit trail.

Primary regulatory or donor compliance framework governing this grant budget (e.g., OMB 2 CFR 200, USAID ADS, DFID Terms and Conditions, UN Financial Rules). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OMB_2_CFR_200|USAID_ADS|DFID_TC|UN_Financial_Rules|EU_FPA|Private_Foundation|Other — promote to reference product]
Required cost-sharing or matching contribution amount that the organization must provide from non-grant sources to meet donor requirements.
Timestamp when this grant budget record was first created in Unit4 ERP system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Approved budget amount allocated to direct costs that can be specifically attributed to the grant program activities (salaries, supplies, travel, equipment).
External reference number or grant agreement number assigned by the donor organization (USAID, DFID, UN agency, private foundation).
Percentage rate applied to calculate indirect costs. Expressed as a percentage of the direct cost base (e.g., 15.00 represents 15%).
Approved budget amount allocated to indirect costs (Facilities and Administration or F&A costs) that support the grant but cannot be directly attributed to specific activities.
Indicates whether this budget spans multiple fiscal years (True) or is contained within a single fiscal year (False).
Date of the most recent approved budget modification or amendment.
Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this grant budget record.
Timestamp when this grant budget record was last modified in Unit4 ERP system.

business_glossary_term: Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) Rate
description: Federally negotiated indirect cost rate established through a Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) with the cognizant federal agency. May differ from the ICR rate applied if donor caps indirect costs.
Total approved funding amount for the grant award. Represents the ceiling for all expenditures under this budget.
Username or identifier of the system user who created this grant budget record.
Primary key for nicra_rate

business_glossary_term: Created By Staff Member Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.staff_member. Business justification: Finance tracks which staff member created each NICRA rate for accountability in indirect cost rate management and compliance.

Foreign key linking to compliance.nicra_agreement. Business justification: Finance nicra_rate table operationalizes rates from compliance nicra_agreement. Finance applies rates; compliance maintains agreement terms. Link ensures rate application matches negotiated agreement

The administrative component of the Facilities and Administration (F&A) rate, if the NICRA separates facilities and administrative costs. This rate covers general administration, departmental administration, and sponsored projects administration.

The official agreement number assigned by the cognizant federal agency to this Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement. This is the externally-known identifier used in grant reporting and compliance documentation.

The current lifecycle status of the NICRA agreement. Draft indicates the agreement is being prepared; pending approval means it has been submitted to the cognizant agency; approved means the agency has accepted it; active means it is currently in effect; expired means the effective period has ended; superseded means a newer agreement has replaced it; terminated means it was cancelled before expiration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|expired|superseded|terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date on which the cognizant federal agency officially approved the NICRA agreement. This may differ from the negotiation date if there was a review period.
The end date of the audit period on which this NICRA rate is based. For final rates, this is the period for which actual costs were audited to establish the rate.
The start date of the audit period on which this NICRA rate is based. For final rates, this is the period for which actual costs were audited to establish the rate.

The dollar amount of carryforward adjustment from prior periods that must be applied when using this rate. Carryforward adjustments occur when provisional rates differ from final rates, requiring reconciliation in subsequent periods.

The code identifying the federal agency responsible for negotiating and approving this indirect cost rate agreement. Common cognizant agencies include USAID, HHS, USDA, and DOD. The cognizant agency is typically the agency providing the most federal funding to the organization.

The full name of the federal agency responsible for negotiating and approving this indirect cost rate agreement (e.g., United States Agency for International Development, Department of Health and Human Services).

The cost base to which the indirect cost rate is applied. Modified Total Direct Costs (MTDC) is the most common base and excludes capital expenditures, equipment, subawards over $25K, and other specific costs. Total Direct Costs includes all direct costs. Salaries and Wages base applies the rate only to personnel costs.
The timestamp when this NICRA rate record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Indicates whether this record represents the use of the de minimis indirect cost rate. Organizations that have never received a negotiated rate may elect to use a de minimis rate of 10% of Modified Total Direct Costs per OMB Uniform Guidance.
URL or file path to the official NICRA agreement document. This provides direct access to the signed agreement for audit and compliance purposes.

The date on which this negotiated indirect cost rate expires and is no longer applicable to federal grants. This is the end of the rate period as specified in the NICRA agreement. Nullable for open-ended agreements pending renegotiation.
The date on which this negotiated indirect cost rate becomes effective and can be applied to federal grants. This is the beginning of the rate period as specified in the NICRA agreement.

The facilities component of the Facilities and Administration (F&A) rate, if the NICRA separates facilities and administrative costs. This rate covers depreciation, operations and maintenance, library, and other facility-related costs.
The fiscal year to which this NICRA rate applies. Format typically YYYY or YYYY-YYYY for multi-year agreements. This helps track which rate applies to which grant period.

The negotiated fringe benefit rate expressed as a percentage, if included in the NICRA agreement. Fringe benefits are often negotiated alongside indirect cost rates and applied to salaries and wages to calculate total personnel costs.

Indicates whether this NICRA rate is currently active and should be used for indirect cost calculations on new grants. True means the rate is within its effective period and approved for use; False means it is expired, superseded, or not yet effective.
The username or identifier of the user who last modified this NICRA rate record in the system. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The timestamp when this NICRA rate record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The date on which the indirect cost rate was formally negotiated and agreed upon with the cognizant federal agency. This is the date the NICRA agreement was signed.

The code identifying the organizational unit (division, department, or legal entity) to which this NICRA rate applies. Some organizations negotiate separate rates for different divisions or subsidiaries.
The name of the organizational unit (division, department, or legal entity) to which this NICRA rate applies.

The functional category or pool to which this rate applies. Organizations may have multiple rate categories such as headquarters operations, field operations, research, or program-specific pools. This allows for different indirect cost rates for different types of activities.

The negotiated indirect cost rate expressed as a percentage. This rate is applied to the applicable cost base to calculate allowable indirect cost recovery on federally-funded grants. For example, a rate of 15.50 means 15.50% of the base can be recovered as indirect costs.

The type of indirect cost rate negotiated with the federal government. Predetermined rates are negotiated in advance for a future period; fixed rates are established for a specific period and not subject to adjustment; provisional rates are temporary rates subject to adjustment; final rates are established after costs are known and audited.

Free-text field capturing any special conditions, limitations, or instructions specified in the NICRA agreement. This may include restrictions on which grants the rate applies to, special calculation methods, or other agency-imposed conditions.
The agreement number of the subsequent NICRA that supersedes or replaces this agreement. Populated when a new rate is negotiated.
Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key for the cost center master record.

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Cost centers (programs/field offices) have specific compliance obligations (CHS commitments, sphere standards, donor-specific requirements). Program managers track obligations by cost center for opera
Reference to the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting from field offices to regional hubs to headquarters. Nullable for top-level cost centers.
Reference to the user who created this cost center record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.333 Record Retention

description: Reference to the staff member who is accountable for budget performance, expense approval, and financial stewardship of this cost center. Used in RACI (Responsible Accountable Consulted Informed) matrices and budget versus actual (BvA) reporting.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 - Financial Management

description: Foreign key linking to technology.system_platform. Business justification: Cost centers track IT platform-specific expenses (ERP licenses, grants management subscriptions) for budget allocation and indirect cost recovery. Required for platform cost reports in NICRA calculati
Reference to the user who approved this cost center for activation. Typically a finance director, controller, or Chief Financial Officer (CFO). Nullable for cost centers not yet approved.

The primary activity or service provided by this cost center (e.g., beneficiary registration, field assessment, grant management, financial reporting, volunteer coordination). Used for activity-based costing and internal service billing.
The total approved budget amount for this cost center for the current fiscal year. Used in Budget versus Actual (BvA) variance analysis and financial stewardship reporting.

Workflow approval status for new or modified cost centers. Draft cost centers are under construction; pending approval cost centers await finance or executive review; approved cost centers are active; rejected cost centers require revision.
The date and time when this cost center was approved for activation. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. Nullable for cost centers not yet approved.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost center's budget and financial reporting. Used for multi-currency organizations with country offices operating in local currencies.

The date and time when this cost center was permanently closed and all balances were transferred or reconciled. Used for grant closeout, project completion, and organizational restructuring. Nullable for active cost centers.
The SAP S/4HANA or Unit4 ERP company code representing the legal entity to which this cost center belongs. Used for multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.
The SAP S/4HANA controlling area (CO module) to which this cost center is assigned. Controlling areas represent organizational units for internal cost accounting and management reporting.

The methodology used to allocate shared or indirect costs to this cost center. Direct allocation assigns costs explicitly; proportional uses a percentage driver; headcount allocates by staff count; square footage uses physical space; activity-based costing (ABC) uses cost drivers; not allocated for cost centers that do not receive shared cost allocations.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.405 Allocable Costs

description: SAP S/4HANA cost center category classification. Production cost centers deliver direct program services; service cost centers provide internal support; administrative cost centers manage overhead; auxiliary cost centers support shared services; project cost centers are time-bound; temporary cost centers are short-term or pilot initiatives.
The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in SAP S/4HANA and Unit4 ERP. Used in general ledger posting, budget tracking, and financial reporting.

Detailed narrative description of the cost center's purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Includes information on program activities, geographic coverage, donor restrictions, and organizational context.
The full descriptive name of the cost center as it appears in financial reports and organizational charts.

Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active cost centers accept expense postings; inactive cost centers are temporarily disabled; suspended cost centers are under review; closed cost centers are permanently retired; pending activation cost centers are awaiting approval.

Classification of the cost center by functional expense category per GAAP for nonprofits. Program cost centers support direct mission delivery; administrative cost centers support organizational management; fundraising cost centers support donor cultivation and gift processing; general and administrative (G&A) support overall operations; mission support provides enabling services; field operations manage country office and site-level activities.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary geographic location of this cost center. Used for country office cost centers, field operations, and regional program management. Nullable for headquarters or global cost centers.
The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.333 Record Retention

description: The functional department or division to which this cost center belongs (e.g., Health Programs, WASH, Education, Finance, Human Resources, Monitoring Evaluation and Learning). Used for cross-cutting organizational reporting.
The date on which this cost center ceases to be active. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used for project-specific or time-bound organizational units.
The date from which this cost center becomes active and can accept expense allocations and budget assignments.

The unique identifier for this cost center in external systems such as donor grant management systems (GMS), partner organization ERPs, or government reporting portals. Used for cross-system reconciliation and International Aid Transparency Initiative (IATI) reporting.

GAAP functional expense category per FASB ASC 958-720. Program services are mission-delivery activities; management and general (M&G) are administrative overhead; fundraising supports donor cultivation; membership development supports constituent engagement. Required for Form 990 and audited financial statements.

Classification of donor-imposed restrictions on funds allocated to this cost center per FASB ASC 958-205. Unrestricted funds have no donor-imposed restrictions; temporarily restricted funds have time or purpose restrictions that will lapse; permanently restricted funds (endowments) must be maintained in perpetuity.

The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents headquarters or top-level divisions; higher numbers represent nested field offices, country offices, or project sites.

Indicates whether expenses posted to this cost center are eligible for indirect cost recovery under the organization's Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA). True for direct program cost centers; false for administrative or already-indirect cost centers. Used in Facilities and Administration (F&A) cost allocation.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.414 Indirect Costs

description: The date and time when this cost center record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.333 Record Retention

business_glossary_term: Negotiated Indirect Cost Rate Agreement (NICRA) Base Category
description: The indirect cost base category used for calculating indirect cost recovery for this cost center per the organization's NICRA with the federal government. Modified Total Direct Costs (MTDC) is the most common base; direct salaries and wages is used for some research organizations; total direct costs includes all direct expenses. Not applicable for cost centers that do not participate in indirect cost allocation.
reference: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200.68 Modified Total Direct Costs

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, donor conditions, compliance requirements, or historical context relevant to this cost center.

The profit center code for organizations using profit center accounting in SAP S/4HANA. Used for segment reporting and internal performance measurement. Nullable for nonprofits not using profit center structures.

The thematic program area or sector this cost center supports (e.g., WASH - Water Sanitation and Hygiene, GBV - Gender-Based Violence, Health, Education, Livelihoods, Emergency Response). Used for Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) alignment and donor reporting.

Geographic region for multi-country or regional cost centers. Used for regional program offices, humanitarian response hubs, and Development Assistance Committee (DAC) reporting. The operational system of record from which this cost center master data originated. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA, Unit4 ERP, and legacy systems.
The cumulative actual expenses posted to this cost center from the start of the fiscal year to the current reporting period. Used in BvA reporting and burn rate analysis.

The frequency at which financial reports must be submitted to the donor or funding source. Drives reporting calendar and compliance workflows.
